_meta.note: Cached demo output for Kaftan Co-ord Set evaluation. Collected 2026-06-14 using live API calls. Served automatically when the default demo inputs are submitted so the app runs reliably without API keys.
_meta.trend_run: Kaftan Co-ord Set, Cotton/Cotton-linen, ₹600–999, Next Buying Cycle

input.buyerNote: Kaftan co-ords trialled last festive season (₹899, rayon, block print) — sold through 61% in 8 weeks across metro stores but moved poorly in Tier 2 and Tier 3 (38%). Category skews strongly occasion-wear: Eid, navratri, and beach/resort gifting drove most units. Reorder velocity was low — customers treat it as a one-off buy, not a wardrobe repeat. Rayon with wide sleeves had fitting complaints post-wash; cotton-modal blend performed better on returns. Our vendor needs 50-day lead on embroidery or block-print work. Max Fashion has started stocking kaftans at ₹699 with solid colours — differentiation needs to come from print story or silhouette detail. Suggest limiting depth to 80–120 units per store for trial; avoid wide size curve below S/M.

creator.evidence_summary: 5 Instagram mentions found via Google Search (site:instagram.com). Content skews resort/vacation styling — posts feature kaftans at beach destinations and staycations. 2 YouTube videos referencing kaftan co-ords in summer haul context, both under 40K views. Creator tone is aspirational, not value-fashion.
creator.what_it_proves: Awareness exists in India — kaftans are being styled and posted. But the creator segment surfaced here skews premium and aspirational, not the ₹600–999 value-fashion buyer.
creator.what_could_mislead: No follower count, niche, or engagement rate available — a 500K resort travel creator and a 10K value-fashion creator look identical in this signal. Aspirational kaftan buzz may not convert to sales at ₹799.

marketplace.evidence_summary: Found 9 listings across Myntra, Amazon.in, Nykaa Fashion. 3 of 9 listings fall within your target band (₹600–999) with 184 reviews in-band. Total reviews across all: 1,042. Avg rating: 3.9. Most listings sit above ₹1,200 (resort/branded kaftan segment). In-band supply is thin but present.
marketplace.what_it_proves: Whether the trend has real commercial supply on Indian marketplaces and how much of it lands within your target price band. In-band listing count + review volume indicate actual purchase activity at your price point.
marketplace.what_could_mislead: Google Shopping ranks by relevance and sponsored placement, not demand strength. Heavy discounting can signal overstock, not velocity. Items outside your band skew the review total.

search_interest.evidence_summary: Google Trends India — "kaftan co-ord set" (last 12 months): peak 62/100, current 41/100, momentum is stable. Top states: Kerala, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Goa. Rising searches: kaftan coord set myntra ↑↑, cotton kaftan set women ↑↑, kaftan set under 1000 ↑↑. Top searches: kaftan co-ord set, kaftan set women, kaftan dress set.
search_interest.what_it_proves: That people in India are actively searching for this trend — and whether that search intent is accelerating or cooling. Rising = buy ahead of peak; declining = markdown risk is rising.
search_interest.what_could_mislead: Search interest is a lagging signal — peaks follow social buzz, not store sales. National interest may not reflect your catchment or value-fashion buyer profile.

competitor.evidence_summary: Max Fashion: 2 kaftan co-ord listings found (₹699 solid colour, ₹799 block print — 1 new arrival). No kaftan co-ord listings found on Ajio at this keyword. Max's solid-colour entry at ₹699 is the direct price-band threat; differentiation must come from print or fabric hand.
competitor.what_it_proves: Max Fashion has validated the kaftan co-ord category at value price points. Their entry confirms retailer conviction but also signals the window for first-mover advantage has passed.
competitor.what_could_mislead: Max Fashion serves a slightly different store catchment than Reliance Trends / Vishal Mega Mart. Their inventory decision may not reflect demand in your specific geography or customer base.

historical.evidence_summary: 2 relevant analogs matched. Closest: Ethnic Fusion Co-ord Sets (A005, 2024) — Ajrakh/Bagru print co-ords at ₹799–1099, moderate sell-through (est. 58%), strong in South India and metro. Secondary: Floral Print Co-ord Sets (A001, 2023) — occasion-wear profile with festive peak, weaker in Tier 2. Both show that occasion-anchored silhouettes sell in bursts, not steadily. Data source: hand-curated dataset — disclosed.
historical.what_it_proves: Commercial precedent by fabric, silhouette, and price band.
historical.what_could_mislead: Dataset is small (10 entries) and hand-curated. Neither analog is a kaftan — both are straight kurta / palazzo silhouettes. Kaftan's wider sleeve and longer hem may behave differently on returns and fit satisfaction.

raw_data.trend: Ethnic Fusion Co-ord Sets (kurta + pallazo in Ajrakh / Bagru print)
raw_data.outcome: Moderate sell-through (est. 58%). Strong in South India and metro Tier-1. Weaker in North Tier-2.
raw_data.what_worked: Print story differentiated from competitor solids. Festive gifting drove initial velocity. Cotton-linen fabric reduced returns vs synthetic.
raw_data.what_didnt: Reorder velocity was low — customers bought one and considered the look 'done'. Wider palazzo sizing was returned more often in North India.
raw_data.notes: Most relevant silhouette analog for kaftan — both are relaxed ethnic occasion-wear with print as the key differentiator.

raw_data.outcome: Strong sell-through in summer (est. 72%). Heavy markdown risk post-July. Tier-2 underperformed vs metro.
raw_data.what_worked: Price band alignment. Summer occasion demand (outings, travel). Multiple colourways kept assortment fresh.
raw_data.what_didnt: Rayon quality complaints in humid regions. Post-monsoon inventory became a markdown trap. Tier-2 buyer preferred solids.
raw_data.notes: Seasonal risk profile is similar to kaftan — strong summer window, hard to clear in monsoon and winter.

disagreement_view: Creator signal and search interest point in the same direction — kaftan awareness is real in India and search momentum peaked in Apr–May, now gently declining. But there is a sharp disagreement between what creators are showing (premium resort kaftans at ₹1,500–₹3,000) and what the buyer's own data shows (61% sell-through last festive season at ₹899, struggling in Tier 2). The marketplace signal confirms this split: only 3 of 9 listings fall within the ₹600–999 band, with the bulk of review volume concentrated in the ₹1,200+ premium segment. The unresolved question is whether the cotton-linen kaftan at ₹799–₹899 with a strong print story occupies a viable middle ground — aspirational enough to attract the creator-influenced metro buyer, functional enough to sell in value-fashion retail. The buyer's note adds a critical ground-truth constraint: reorder velocity is low. That single fact changes the calculus — this is a one-time buy at best, which means depth commitment should stay conservative even if the entry signal is positive.

india_fit.climate: Reasonable fit for Apr–Sep. Kaftan silhouette is loose and breathable — cotton-linen is the correct fabric choice. Avoid rayon (humidity + wash complaints confirmed in buyer note). Post-monsoon inventory risk is real — plan sell-through by mid-August.
india_fit.modesty_norms: Good fit. Full-length kaftan with wide sleeves satisfies modesty requirements for urban South Indian, Muslim, and semi-conservative North Indian buyers. Sleeve width and hem length must be maintained — cropped kaftan variants will reduce the addressable buyer pool significantly.
india_fit.price_sensitivity: ₹699–₹899 is the viable window. Below ₹699, fabric quality perception drops. Above ₹999, the buyer switches to premium channels (Nykaa Fashion, Myntra branded). Max Fashion has already staked ₹699 — your entry needs either the same price with better print, or ₹799 with clear print differentiation.
india_fit.occasion_relevance: Occasion-anchored, not daily-wear. Eid, Navratri, beach/resort gifting are the primary demand triggers — buyer note confirms this. This limits repeat purchase velocity. Plan assortment around the festive calendar (Aug–Oct 2026 is the next window given next-cycle buying horizon).
india_fit.overall: Moderate India value-fashion fit. The silhouette works for occasion-wear in metro and coastal markets. The risk is low reorder velocity and a narrow seasonal window — this is a bet on one festive cycle, not a staple.

suggested_units: 80–120 units per store (initial order). Cotton-linen only — no rayon. Skew toward block print or ethnic print to differentiate from Max Fashion's solid-colour offering. Size curve: concentrate on S/M/L, cap XL+ at 10%. Prioritise metro and coastal stores (Mumbai, Bengaluru, Kochi, Chennai) for first deployment.
evidence_gaps: Creator audience demographics unknown — cannot confirm surfaced creators reach value-fashion buyers at ₹600–999
Tier 2 and Tier 3 kaftan performance is uncertain — buyer note confirms last season's 38% sell-through in Tier 2, but root cause (wrong price? wrong print? wrong occasion fit?) is not diagnosed
Fabric return rate data missing — buyer note flags rayon complaints but no data on cotton-linen return rates specifically
Festive timing confirmation needed — next buying cycle (31–90 days from June) maps to Aug–Sep, which includes Eid and early Navratri; verify Eid date and regional relevance for your store catchment
No stockout data from Myntra or Max Fashion — cannot tell if low in-band listing count means low supply or high sell-through
recommended_actions: Limit initial depth to 80–120 units per store — do not widen until week-4 sell-through data is available
Source cotton-linen only; reject rayon samples at vendor stage (buyer note confirms wash/fit complaints)
Invest in print story differentiation — Max Fashion holds ₹699 solids; your SKU must justify ₹799–₹899 with a block print or ethnic print design
Target metro coastal stores first (Mumbai, Bengaluru, Kochi) — search interest and historical analog both confirm South India as the stronger market
Set a hard 4-week sell-through checkpoint: reorder if above 55%, markdown to ₹599 if below 35%, exit if below 25%
caveats: Reorder velocity for kaftan category is structurally low — buyer note confirms customers treat it as a one-off purchase. Do not project repeat sales into your unit economics.
Summer search peak (Apr–May) has already passed. The next demand window is Eid/Navratri (Aug–Oct). Inventory must be in store by late July.
Max Fashion's entry at ₹699 solid compresses the margin window — any price-matching exercise will erode contribution margin. Differentiate on print or accept the margin hit.
Creator signal quality is unverified — no follower count or engagement data available. Aspirational kaftan content does not guarantee value-fashion purchase conversion.
Historical analogs (A005, A001) are kurta/palazzo silhouettes, not kaftans. Kaftan's wider sleeve may increase fit complaints and returns compared to analog data suggests.
